title: 7 income tax limits every taxpayer should know to avoid notices and penalties
summary: Tax compliance goes beyond filing your income tax return on time. Several provisions under the Income Tax Act, 1961, set limits on cash transactions and high-value financial dealings. Staying within these limits can help taxpayers avoid penalties, scrutiny and unnecessary legal complications.

title: PAN-Aadhaar linking for ITR filing: Why it matters and how to complete the process online
summary: PAN-Aadhaar linking is essential for eligible taxpayers to keep their PAN active and avoid disruptions in tax-related services. Taxpayers with unlinked PANs can complete the process by paying the applicable fee through the e-filing portal.&nbsp;

title: Our inflation and interest rates now depend more on monsoon than crude oil
summary: With crude prices cooling after the West Asia truce, the monsoon—and its impact on food inflation—may become the biggest factor shaping RBI's interest rate decisions in the months ahead.

title: India–UK trade deal to make imported Scotch whisky cheaper: Prices may drop 5–10% for Indian consumers
summary: The India–UK FTA is set to come into effect on 15 July. One of the biggest takeaways for Indian consumers is the gradual reduction in prices of imported Scotch whisky and gin. title: ITR utility updated for AY 2026-27: New reporting field added for gifts and rural agricultural land sale receipts
summary: For AY 2026-27, the earlier ‘Other Exempt Income’ field in Schedule EI has been removed. In its place, the department has now introduced a new reporting option titled ‘Receipts not in the nature of income.’

title: New EPF Scheme 2026: Know withdrawal frequency limits for essential, housing and special needs
summary: Under the EPF Scheme, 2026, withdrawals have been simplified into three categories: essential needs, housing needs and special circumstances.

title: EPF Scheme 2026: Newly married members must update nomination, but spouse inclusion is not compulsory
summary: Under the EPF Scheme 2026, members must submit a fresh nomination after marriage, as earlier nominations become invalid. While nominating a spouse is optional, updating nominations ensures eligible family members receive EPF benefits without delays or legal complications.

title: 8th Pay Commission: Experts decode the fitment factor range and why allowances may not offset a lower multiplier
summary: The 8th Pay Commission is reviewing salaries, pensions and allowances for central government employees and pensioners, with the fitment factor remaining the key focus. Experts expect it to range between 1.90 and 2.86.
